flashcards.back: Probably Approximately Correct - framework for learning with high probability and bounded error
Maximum number of points a hypothesis class can shatter. Measures model complexity.

flashcards.front: What is the significance of finite VC dimension?
flashcards.back: Ensures PAC learnability through bounded sample complexity
Find hypothesis h with P[error(h) ≤ ε] ≥ 1-δ using polynomial samples
Hypothesis class can perfectly separate all possible labelings of a point set

flashcards.front: Formula for VC dimension sample complexity
flashcards.back: m = O((d + log(1/δ))/ε²) where d=VC dimension

flashcards.front: Key difference between PAC and agnostic learning
flashcards.back: Agnostic allows no perfect classifier (realizable vs non-realizable)
Bounds growth function: Π_H(m) ≤ (em/d)^d for VC-dim d

flashcards.front: VC dimension of linear classifiers in ℝ²

flashcards.back: Maximum number of labelings achievable by H on m points
